Brand System · Version 1.0

PDAM
Datastream Prism

A visual identity for a personal digital asset manager built around clarity, ownership, organization, and secure movement of digital content.

Foundation Logo Color Typography Imagery Product UI Usage Rules
01 · Foundation

Organized assets. Secure ownership. Effortless flow.

The PDAM identity should feel capable and technical without becoming cold. The prism represents many asset types converging into one organized system, while the segmented geometry suggests synchronization, versioning, and modularity.

Prismatic

Faceted geometry communicates that every digital asset can be understood from multiple perspectives: file, version, creator, license, and backup.

Controlled

Dark surfaces, disciplined spacing, and restrained accents reinforce security, ownership, and operational confidence.

Fluid

Purple-to-blue motion gradients evoke data moving cleanly between local storage, hosted services, and external backups.

02 · Logo System

The prism mark

Use the standalone mark for application icons, favicons, social avatars, and compact navigation. Pair it with the PDAM wordmark in marketing and onboarding contexts.

Clear space

Maintain clear space equal to roughly one quarter of the mark’s width on every side. No text, controls, borders, or other marks should enter this area.

Minimum size

Use the full-color mark at no less than 32 px digitally or 9 mm in print. Below that size, use a simplified flat-color variant.

03 · Color

Prismatic core palette

Purple is the primary brand signal. Blue and cyan support states related to synchronization, storage, connectivity, and progress. Neutral surfaces should carry most of the interface.

Prism Violet#7E3FF2
Facet Magenta#A855F7
Stream Indigo#6366F1
Sync Blue#3B82F6
Signal Cyan#0EA5E9

Primary surfaces

#090D14 for the page background and #121722 for panels.

Primary text

#F8FAFC on dark surfaces and #111827 on light surfaces.

Muted text

#A7B0C0 on dark surfaces. Maintain WCAG contrast for all functional text.

04 · Typography

Technical, clean, and readable

Display · Space Grotesk Semibold
Your assets.
Organized.
Interface and body · Inter Regular / Medium

Use Inter for product UI, metadata, settings, tables, and long-form documentation. Prefer sentence case, concise labels, and generous line height. Reserve all caps for short utility labels and section markers.

Code and identifiers · JetBrains Mono

asset_01J8PDAM9T7A2 · sha256 · v2.4.1

05 · Iconography & Imagery

Show the work, not generic technology

Iconography

1

Use simple geometric line icons with rounded joins and consistent 1.5–2 px strokes.

2

Favor recognizable metaphors: cube for assets, stacked layers for versions, database cylinder for storage, and flowing nodes for sync.

3

Use color sparingly. Most icons should remain neutral until selected, active, or associated with a status.

Product imagery

1

Show real asset previews, organized libraries, version histories, backup targets, and creator workflows.

2

Use subdued environments so colorful asset thumbnails remain the focal point.

3

Avoid abstract stock imagery of clouds, locks, or glowing server racks unless used as subtle background texture.

06 · Product UI

Dark by default, content first

The application should prioritize asset previews and operational status. Brand gradients are accents, not structural backgrounds.

PDAM

Spacing

Use an 8 px base grid. Favor 16, 24, and 32 px intervals for components and sections.

Radius

Use 10–14 px for controls and cards, 18–24 px for large containers and promotional surfaces.

Motion

Keep transitions between 140–220 ms. Use directional movement to reinforce uploads, sync, and version changes.

07 · Usage Rules

Protect recognition and consistency

Do

  • Use the mark on calm, high-contrast surfaces.
  • Preserve the original proportions and orientation.
  • Use the full gradient for hero moments and key brand touchpoints.
  • Use a flat violet or white version at very small sizes.
  • Keep interface copy direct, practical, and ownership-focused.

Do not

  • Stretch, skew, rotate, or redraw individual facets.
  • Place the mark over busy imagery without a containing surface.
  • Apply unrelated gradients, heavy bevels, or rainbow effects.
  • Use the glow as a substitute for adequate contrast.
  • Fill every interface element with brand purple.
PDAM Brand Guidelines · Datastream Prism Concept · Version 1.0
